updates.note: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) conducted fresh searches at **nine premises** across Kolkata and Murshidabad in connection with its money laundering and **land-grabbing probe** linked to history-sheeter **Biswajit Poddar (Sona Pappu)** and former Kolkata Police DCP **Shantanu Sinha Biswas**. The raids targeted properties of close associates, including a Kolkata Police sub-inspector and a Murshidabad civic body vice-chairperson, as part of investigation into forced land acquisitions. The status of this commitment remains in progress as central and state investigative procedures continue.

The West Bengal government officially granted formal permission to the Enforcement Directorate (ED) to **prosecute ex-RG Kar Medical College principal Sandip Ghosh** in connection with **alleged financial irregularities and corruption**. Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari announced the decision, noting that previous administrative delays in prosecuting Ghosh have been removed to expedite the probe.

On May 18, 2026, the state cabinet officially approved the formation of a **high-level inquiry commission** to investigate **institutional corruption, bribery, cut money, and financial mismanagement** across central and state social and construction projects. Led by retired Calcutta High Court **Justice Biswajit Basu** and member-secretary **K. Jayaraman** (ADG, North Bengal), the commission will commence operations on June 1, 2026.

The administration announced that the commission is expected to provide recommendations within 30 days of functioning to initiate criminal action under the **Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita**.

Former West Bengal minister **Rathin Ghosh** appeared before the Enforcement Directorate (ED) at the CGO Complex in Salt Lake for questioning in connection with the **multi-crore civic recruitment scam**. Ghosh was interrogated regarding alleged illegal appointments made in various municipalities during his tenure as the chairman of **Madhyamgram Municipality**.

The Enforcement Directorate (ED) arrested Kolkata Police Deputy Commissioner **Shantanu Sinha Biswas** in connection with a money laundering probe linked to a criminal syndicate run by **Sona Pappu**. Biswas, a former officer-in-charge of **Kalighat Police Station**, was taken into custody after over 10 hours of interrogation following his evasion of multiple summons.

Former West Bengal Minister **Sujit Basu** was arrested by the Enforcement Directorate (ED) in connection with the **South Dumdum Municipality recruitment scam**. Officials alleged that Basu recommended around 150 candidates for posts in exchange for financial benefits during the 2014-2016 period of the TMC regime. Direct proceeds of crime, including flats and cash deposits, have been identified.
